impact:
Shares a root cause with ISS-001 (gap A) and ISS-002 (gap B) — drive-dependent clip behaviour. Solve once.

Original: V2 zener drive tracking — knee/softness needs drive-dependence; D0.90 THD imbalance.

2026-07-16 (cascade_analysis.py section B, V2 BL=1.00 captures only — clean of the ISS-008 dry-path error): V2's wet path shows the same drive-dependent signature as V1E does: twin-T 800Hz region swings 7.03dB across DRIVE (D0.25 +4.27 -> D0.50 +2.06 -> D0.90 -2.75) while matching within ~2dB at low drive; mid 1-2.5k swings 3.75dB; LF swings 3.92dB. As on V1E, the filters are correct at low drive and only the drive-dependence is wrong.

Note the sign differs between revisions (V1E goes strongly negative at high drive, V2 goes mildly negative from a positive start), so this is NOT one shared constant — but it is one shared MECHANISM (how the clip stage's effective gain/curvature tracks level and frequency). V1L/V2 share the zener drive module (ZenerDriveClipRecovery + ZenerPairT); V1E is rail-clip-only. So a fix may be per-clip-topology while the diagnosis is shared.

Prerequisite: V2's zener Cj/knee are still provisional (v2Params() == v1LateParams(), per CLAUDE.md) and kOutputMakeup[2] is an admitted placeholder (ISS-008). Fitting the zener against a wrong output makeup risks another two-errors-cancelling. Recommend fixing ISS-008 first even though the BL=1.00 captures used here are themselves clean.
title: V2 zener drive tracking: THD imbalance across frequency
